Label: AL rdfs:label@en
Definition: AL IAO:0000115@en

Label: lowland
Definition: A landform which has an elevation lower than 200m above sea level.

Label: plant community
Definition: An environmental system which consists of a set of plants at some stage in the community assembly process.

Label: ecosystem functional group
Definition: A group of related ecosystems within a biome that share common ecological drivers, which in turn promote similar biotic traits that characterise the group.

Label: tropical-subtropical lowland rainforests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a rainforest plant community, experiences tropical climactic conditions and is located in a lowland landform.

Label: tropical dry forests and thickets
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a forest plant community, experiences tropical climactic conditions and seasonally dry conditions.

Label: forest plant community
Definition: A plant community which consists mainly of plants with a tree growth form, possessing a continuous and closed canopy.

Label: rainforest plant community
Definition: A plant community which consists mainly of plants with a tree growth form, possessing a continuous and closed canopy, is subject to high humidity and rainfall and includes populations of epiphytes, lianas and vegetation adapted to high moisture.

Label: seasonally dry
Definition: An environmental condition in which an area receives ~1800 mm of rainfall per year, with a period of 3 to 6 months receiving less than 100 mm per month.

Label: subtropical dry forests and thickets
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a forest plant community, experiences subtropical climactic conditions and seasonally dry conditions.

Label: subtropical
Definition: An environmental condition in which an area experiences hot summers and mild winters, and is located to the immediate north or south of the tropical zone.

Label: tropical montane rainforests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a rainforest plant community, experiences tropical climactic conditions and is located at a montane altitude.

Label: subtropical montane rainforests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a rainforest plant community, experiences subtropical conditions and is located at a montane altitude.

Label: subtropical lowland rainforests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a rainforest plant community, experiences subtropical climactic conditions and is located in a lowland landform.

Label: heath plant community
Definition: A plant community which consists mainly of open, low-growing woody vegetation.

Label: tropical heath forests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has some heath and some forest plant communities and experiences tropical climactic conditions.

Label: temperate high montane forests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which consists of a forest plant community, experiences temperate climactic conditions and is located in an upper montane region.

Label: deciduous plant community
Definition: A plant community dominated by broadleaf deciduous trees.

Label: deciduous temperate forests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a broadleaf forest plant community and experiences temperate climactic conditions.

Label: oceanic cool temperate rainforests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a rainforest plant community and experiences oceanic temperate climate conditions.

Label: laurel forest
Definition: A plant community characterized by broadleaf tree species with evergreen, glossy, elongated leaves.

Label: warm temperate laurophyll forests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a laurel forest plant community and experiences temperate climactic conditions.

Label: fire-adapted ecosystem
Definition: An ecosystem which has a history of regular fires that reset the seccessional state of the ecosystem and vegetation adapted to survive or require regular fire.

Label: humid temperate
Definition: A temperate condition which features frequent precipitation and humid conditions.

Label: temperate pyric humid forests
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a forest plant community, experiences wet temperate conditions and has vegetation that is adapted to cycles of fire.

Label: sclerophyll forest plant community
Definition: A forest plant community that consists mainly of sclerophyll woody plants, which are drought-adapted and feature stiff, leathery leaves.

Label: temperate pyric sclerophyll forests and woodlands
Definition: An ecosystem functional group which has a sclerophyll forest plant community, experiences temperate climactic conditions and has vegetation that is adapted to cycles of fire.

Label: shrubland plant community
Definition: A plant community characterized by vegetation dominated by shrubs that includes grasses, herbs and geophytes.

Label: seasonally dry tropical shrublands
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that experiences seasonally dry and tropical climactic conditions, and consists of a shrubland vegetation community.

Label: seasonally dry temperate heathlands
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that experiences seasonally dry and temperate climactic conditions, and consists of a heath plant community.

Label: seasonally dry temperate shrublands
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that experiences seasonally dry and temperate climactic conditions, and consists of a shrubland plant community.

Label: cool temperate
Definition: A temperate condition which features cool climactic conditions.

Label: cool temperate heathlands
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that experiences cool temperate climactic conditions and consists of a heath plant community.

Label: rocky pavements, screes and lava flows
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that is characterized by exposure to extreme temperatures and weathering, with shallow soils and vegetation dominated by cryptogams (lichens, bryophytes)
editors note: This class has no axioms because it seems to be an outlier, but they may be added if other types would use them as well. -TA

Label: trophic savannas
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that contains grassy woodlands and grasslands kept short by an abundance and diversity of large herbivores.

Label: pyric tussock savannas
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that contains grassy woodlands and grasslands kept short by recurring fires.

Label: savanna plant community
Definition: A plant community which consists mainly of grasses and scattered woody vegetation.

Label: hummock savannas
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that contains a savanna plant community dominated by hummock-forming grasses.

Label: temperate woodlands
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that contains temperate climactic conditions, and consists of a woodland plant community.

Label: woodland plant community
Definition: A plant community which consists mainly of trees and shrubs forming an open canopy.

Label: temperate subhumid
Definition: A temperate condition which features dry winters and wet summers.

Label: temperate subhumid grasslands
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that experiences temperate subhumid climactic conditions and has a grassland plant community.

Label: grassland plant community
Definition: A plant community dominated by grasses with interstitial forbs and isolated trees or shrubs.

Label: semi-desert steppe
Definition: An ecosystem functional group characterized by a semi-arid climactic condition consisting of a grassland plant community.

Label: semi-arid
Definition: An environmental condition in which annual precipitation is less than the precipitation threshold of an area, but more than 50% of the threshold.

Label: succulent deserts
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that consists mainly of succulent plant community and experiences arid climactic conditions.

Label: succulent semi-deserts
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that consists mainly of succulent plant community and experiences semi-arid climactic conditions.

Label: succulent plant community
Definition: A plant community which consists mainly of cactus-like plants or other slow-growing spiny and succulent species.

Label: sclerophyll hot deserts
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that consists of a sclerophyll plant community and experiences arid climactic conditions.

Label: sclerophyll hot semi-deserts
Definition: An ecosystem functional group that consists of a sclerophyll plant community and experiences semi-arid climactic conditions.

Label: sclerophyll plant community
Definition: A plant community that consists mainly of hard-leaved (sclerophyll) plants, which are drought-adapted and feature stiff, leathery leaves.

Label: climatic domain
Definition: A climatic domain is a set of climatic conditions that affect a large portion of the earth.

Label: cryocratic domain
Definition: A climatic domain which consists of severe cold such as the type experienced around the poles and in the high mountains.

Label: mesocratic domain
Definition: A climatic domain which consists of thermic seasons, one cold and another warm.

Label: xerocratic domain
Definition: A climatic domain where the scarcity of moisture is the main determinant factor for living beings.

Label: thermocratic domain
Definition: A climatic domain which has an absence of cold and thermic seasons.
